candidates.why: Explicit top-line revenue label; matches definition of total revenue/sales and is the primary revenue figure.
This is a cost line, not revenue; semantically incorrect for revenue despite being in candidate list.
Represents income from associates, not operating revenue; should not be used as total revenue.
Financial income, not operating revenue from core operations.
Interest income is a financial item, not core revenue.
Net finance result, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Total comprehensive income for the period
candidates.why: Bottom-line performance including OCI, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Excl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Earnings measure, not revenue.

variables.notes: Selected the explicit top-line revenue row 'Total Revenue' as the primary revenue measure.

candidates.why: Direct production/material costs; appropriately represents cost of goods sold/cost of sales.

Label explicitly says COGS and shares values with 'Cost of materials' in at least one year; appears to be an alternate or duplicate label. Either would represent COGS; prefer the more descriptive primary component 'Cost of materials'.
Likely operating payroll (SG&A) rather than direct COGS; not chosen for COGS.
Travel is overhead (SG&A), not direct cost of goods sold.
Investor relations/IR, typically SG&A, not COGS.
Overhead occupancy cost, not direct COGS.
Net operating income/expense line, not specifically COGS.
Financing costs, not COGS.
Financing costs, not COGS.
Net financial result, not COGS.
Net financial result, not COGS.
Balance sheet asset; not an income statement expense.
Inventory component on balance sheet; not the period COGS expense line.

candidates.row_label: Accrued expenses and other current liabilities
candidates.why: Liability, not COGS expense line.

variables.notes: Mapped COGS to the income statement row 'Cost of materials', which clearly represents direct production/material costs. The alternate label 'Cost of Revenues/COGS' appears to be a duplicate; using only one label avoids redundancy.

candidates.why: Net operating income/expense line; may include mixed items and is not clearly an SG&A component. Prefer more specific operating cost components when available.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Brands, Patents, Trademarks, Marketing & Artistic Intangibles
candidates.why: Amortization expense is D&A and should be excluded from SG&A per instructions.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. The provided SG&A candidates are either mixed/net items or D&A, which should be excluded. Instead, I selected specific operating overhead components from the income statement: 'Payroll expense', 'Operating cost', 'Travel expenses', 'Transaction cost -Acquisition-related cost', 'IR expenses', 'Marketing, freight, and commissions', 'Other operating cost excluding transactions cost - Balancing value', and 'Rental Expense'. These appear to be SG&A-type costs (personnel, marketing, travel, other operating expenses) and exclude obvious COGS, depreciation, amortization, and financial items. However, the statement structure is somewhat complex (e.g., presence of balancing values and multiple operating cost lines), so the exact SG&A composition may need confirmation. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Payroll expense, [Income Statement] Operating cost, [Income Statement] Travel expenses, [Income Statement] Transaction cost -Acquisition-related cost, [Income Statement] IR expenses, [Income Statement] Marketing, freight, and commissions, [Income Statement] Other operating cost excluding transactions cost - Balancing value, [Income Statement] Rental Expense

candidates.why: Represents an R&D-related balance sheet item (likely capitalized R&D), not a period R&D expense in the income statement.

variables.notes: No clear research and development EXPENSE line appears in the income statement. The only R&D-related row is a balance sheet item, which should not be used as XRD. Therefore XRD is treated as missing (0).

candidates.why: Primary interest expense line in the income statement; best representation of interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest component of lease liabilities; a specific type of interest expense and suitable as a fallback if main interest expense is missing for some years.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest; good tertiary fallback if other interest lines are unavailable.
Balance sheet liability; not an expense and thus not appropriate for XINT.
Balance sheet liability; not an expense line.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Interest expense is mapped to the primary income statement line 'Interest Expenses', with lease-related interest lines provided as secondary fallbacks, respecting the priority rule for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Investments in associates accounted for using equity method
candidates.why: An asset, not an equity account; not suitable for book equity.
Represents total equity; however there are duplicate labels and a more standard 'Total equity' line that appears to be the main equity total.
Primary total shareholders' equity line on the balance sheet. No separate parent/owner vs non-controlling breakdown is present, so this best represents book equity attributable to owners.
Technical balancing row, not an actual equity balance; should not be used.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Technical balancing row for the accounting equation, not equity itself.
Represents total assets/liabilities+equity, not equity alone.

variables.notes: No separate line for non-controlling interests or equity attributable to owners is provided. 'Total equity' is therefore used as book equity, and MIB will be set to zero to avoid double counting.

No balance sheet line for minority interest or non-controlling interests is present (e.g., no 'Non-controlling interests' or similar). MIB is therefore set to an empty list, implying zero non-controlling interests.
